entries.definition:
كتاب الكاف

كنع - كنف

(كوي) الكاف والواو والياء أصلٌ صحيح، وهو كَوَيْتُ بالنَّار. وقد ذكرناه.

كُلِّ مَكَانٍ and waves come on them from every side II [interjection]
‘stop where you are!’, ‘no more of that!’ (10:28) وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ
نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا مَكَانَكُمْ أَنْتُمْ وَشُرَكَاؤُكُمْ on the Day We gather them all
together, We will say to those who associate partners with God,
‘Stay in your place, you and your partner-gods.’

مَكَانَة makānatun [n.] 1 place (36:67) وَلَوْ نَشَاءُ لَمَسَخْنَاهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِهِمْ فَمَا
اسْتَطَاعُوا مُضِيًّا وَلَا يَرْجِعُونَ and had We willed, We would have
transmuted them where they stood, so that they could not move
forward nor return 2 status, rank, position, ability, power
(11:121) وَقُلْ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ اعْمَلُوا عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنَّا عَامِلُونَ say to those who
do not believe, ‘Act according to your power: We too are [so]
acting.’
entries.definition_english:

‘stop where you are!’, ‘no more of that!’ (10:28) وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ

entries.definition:
k–w–y to burn, to brand, to cauterise, to sting; hot iron;
aperture, small round window. Of this root, تُكْوَى tukwā occurs
once in the Qur’an.

تُكْوَى tukwā [pass. imperf. of v. كَوَىٰ kawā] to be seared, to be
branded, to be cauterised (9:35) يَوْمَ يُحْمَىٰ عَلَيْهَا فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوَىٰ بِهَا
جِبَاهُهُمْ وَجُنُوبُهُمْ وَظُهُورُهُمْ on the day when it [the hoarded gold and
silver] will be heated in the fire of Hell, and their foreheads, sides
and backs will be branded by them.

كَيْ kay [particle indicating reason (حرف تعليل), occurring 10 times in
the Qur’an] so that. This particle precedes a verb in the
subjunctive (منصوب) (20:40) فَرَجَعْنَاكَ إِلَىٰ أُمِّكَ كَيْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا وَلَا تَحْزَنَ so
We returned you to your mother, that she may be tranquil and not
grieve. It is often preceded by prep. ل for emphasis (3:153) فَأَثَابَكُمْ
غَمًّا بِغَمٍّ لِكَيْلَا تَحْزَنُوا عَلَىٰ مَا فَاتَكُمْ وَلَا مَا أَصَابَكُمْ so He requited you with
grief for [the previous] grief [also interpreted as: He rewarded
you with grief upon grief] so that you may not sorrow for what
you missed or for what has happened to you.
